title: Baker's Coast
title_candidates: A Baker's Dozen, Held in Kent
theme: Jon Baker as wire-to-wire metronome on Kent's links — the only flicker of doubt arrived briefly in Round 2 and was snuffed out at Littlestone with one of the great rounds in TEG history.
tone: house — Ronay/Peck, faithful and tongue-in-cheek, written for insiders who were there.
opening_hook: Open on Littlestone, Round 3: Gregg Williams has just drawn level on the first green and the Trophy is suddenly alive — then Jon Baker proceeds to shoot +8 gross, the second-best Gross round in TEG history, and the tournament effectively ends in the Romney Marshes. Then flash back to Deal.
foreshadow: TEG's first foray into Kent — four new links, no local knowledge, plenty of wind
Jon's 9-shot Gross lead after R1 looked decisive; the Trophy race did not agree
John Patterson, a man capable of an eagle and a nine in the same week — sometimes the same round
David Mullin, comfortably mid-pack for three days, with one hole still to come

competitions.name: The Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.winner_or_loser: Jon Baker (158 pts, by 6 from Gregg Williams)
competitions.how: Jon led from R1 (39 pts, +6) but the cushion shrank to a single point after R2 as Gregg closed; the decisive sequence is Gregg drawing level on the 1st at Littlestone (b48), and Jon's response — a 44-point round built on a historic +8 gross (b03, b24) — which restretched the lead to 5 and effectively put the trophy out of reach. R4 was administration: 39 pts, lead out to 6.

competitions.name: The Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.winner_or_loser: Jon Baker (+50, by 18 from Gregg Williams)
competitions.how: Never not leading. +9 after R1, +7 after R2, +14 after R3 thanks to the +8 round at Littlestone (b03), and out to +18 by the end at Prince's. Zero lead changes across the four days — a procession.

competitions.name: The Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: For three rounds the Spoon belonged to John Patterson (R1, R2) and briefly Alex Baker (R3) — Mullin sat a comfortable mid-pack 3rd-3rd-4th. Then on the 10th at Prince's Shore/Dunes in R4 he made a nine (b10), which dropped him to the bottom of the race (b12) and there he stayed. A late, slow-motion trapdoor.

headline_candidates: Deal Or No Deal: Baker Pockets Nine at Royal Cinque Ports
Patterson's Long Walk on a Short Coast
The Wind, the Wedge and the Wooden Spoon Carousel
rounds.chosen_headline: Deal Or No Deal: Baker Pockets Nine at Royal Cinque Ports
rounds.angle: Jon opens with 39 pts and a 9-shot Gross cushion on a course that hosted two Opens; John Patterson racks up three quadruple bogeys and the Spoon is already passing hands like a hot potato.

headline_candidates: The Lead Gets Squeezed at Sandwich
Dunes, Himalayas and a One-Point Margin
rounds.angle: Gregg Williams closes within one and briefly nudges level; Jon answers with a birdie at the last (b35) to restore outright lead heading to Littlestone. Patterson and Mullin contribute the day's blow-ups.

headline_candidates: Patterson Posts 49 — And Still Isn't Close
rounds.angle: The pivotal day. Gregg draws level on the 1st (b48); Jon then shoots the second-best Gross round in TEG history (+8, b03) en route to 44 pts. John Patterson, of all people, posts a personal-best gross +12 and 49 Stableford points — the third-best round in TEG history — and gains barely anything on the leader.

headline_candidates: Patterson Eagles a Par-5, Then Remembers He's Patterson
rounds.angle: Jon closes it out with 39 pts (lead out to 6 on the Trophy, 18 on the Jacket). The day's drama is Mullin's nine at the 10th (b10) which delivers him the Spoon (b12); Alex Baker posts a personal-best gross +17 (b07); Patterson eagles the 2nd (b02) on the way to yet another quadruple bogey.

players.arc: Wire-to-wire in both Trophy and Jacket; the Littlestone +8 (b03) is the round of the tournament and arguably the round of his TEG career.
The only man to lay a glove on Jon — closed to one after R2, drew level on the 1st at Littlestone, then watched the leader disappear into the Romney Marshes. Runner-up in both Trophy and Jacket.

A walking weather system: four quadruple bogeys, a quintuple, an eagle on the 2nd in R4, AND the third-best Stableford round in TEG history (49 pts) with a personal-best gross +12 at Littlestone. Everything, all at once.
Quietly grafted to a personal-best gross +17 in the final round (b07); briefly held the Spoon in R3 before Mullin obliged.
Mid-pack for three rounds, then a nine at the 10th at Prince's Shore/Dunes (b10) and the Spoon was his.

venue_notes: TEG's first visit to Kent — every course a debut. Frame the report as a tour of the south-east links: Royal Cinque Ports (two-time Open host, exposed at Deal) for R1; Prince's Dunes/Himalayas at Sandwich for R2 (27-hole site, future 2030 Walker Cup); Littlestone on the Romney Marshes for R3 (Laidlaw Purves of Royal St George's, refined by Braid and MacKenzie — fittingly the stage for the tournament's defining round); Prince's Shore/Dunes for R4 (the strongest routing, and where the Spoon was settled). Use Kent and the wind as connective tissue, not decoration.
